Large language model
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia


A large language model (LLM) is a type of language model notable for its ability to achieve general-purpose language generation and other natural language processing tasks. LLMs acquire these abilities by learning statistical relationships from vast amounts of text during a self-supervised and semi-supervised training process.


[Architecture Section]
Most modern LLMs use the Transformer architecture. Some also implement Mixture of Experts (MoE).

def example_code():
    """This is a sample with deep indentation"""
    if True:
        print('This must stay indented')
